rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CAROLINE MARGARET MEHEGAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues may include: 1) Accounting begins from June of 2017, but Decedent died in May of 1971. Administrator alleges that he does not have, and there is no way to obtain records from date of death. Administrator also alleges that the original beneficiaries of the estate all executed waivers of accounting, which waive the necessity of an accounting through the last executor's death in 2018. 2) Administrator states that he and his attorney are declining to take statutory fees from this estate and will instead take their fees from the Virginia Mehegan Trust, which will eventually receive the assets herein. 3) Administrator states that he has no knowledge of whether a final income tax return for the Decedent was prepared and filed by the co-executors but is aware that executor Virginia Mehegan filed fiduciary returns prepared and filed during her tenure. 4) Notice was not given to the either the Department of Health Services or the California Victims Compensation Board and no allegation has been made as to whether Decedent received services or if an heir was ever incarcerated. Attorney asserts that no notice was needed at the time letters were first issued in 1971 as PC 9202 was enacted in 1990. Attorney has not provided authority to show that notice is not required if letters were issued prior to the enactment of a statute. Attorney cites PC 9203, but that code section states that if distribution is made prior to the expiration of time to file a claim, a public entity has a claim against the distributee. 5) Petitioner requests that a specific bequest in the amount of $500 carry interest of 4% from 1 year after Decedent's death (1971) pursuant to then Probate Code 162. Attorney cites a case (26 pages; not reviewed) rather than the Probate Code 162 language and proof of its effect in 1971. It is noted that the decedent in the case cited died in 1959. Remote appearances are highly encouraged. Please review the 6th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 6th amended general order.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE JOHN C COX TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #3, REPORT, FEES
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 09/22/2020. The following issues must be addressed: 1) The total amount of the trust and conservatorship funds held by the San Francisco Federal Credit Union exceeds $330,000. Does the FDIC limit apply separately to the trust and conservatorship funds? 2) The Securities America account statement shows cash exceeding $380,000. Is the entire cash amount covered by the SIPC and/or FDIC? 3) Has the petitioner in his trustee capacity requested authority to pay allowances to the beneficiary from the trust estate? 4) Exhibit A is not attached to the petition. 5) Exhibit B is not attached to the petition. A copy of the attorney's invoice is attached without identification. 6) The petition alleges that 21.34% of the portfolio is cash, but according the account statement, the cash is only 13.81%. 7) Is Richard Flahavan no longer a beneficiary of the trust? 8) The summary of account shows an incorrect ending date of the account. 9) Is RailRoad retirement pension an asset of the trust? 10) Has the petitioner in his trustee capacity requested authority to pay gifts approved in the related conservatorship case from the trust estate? 10) Are the two disbursements described as "legal fees" on 04/23/19 reimbursement of costs to the petitioner's attorneys? 11) No statement of Legacy Treasury Direct account is on file. *The petitioner and his attorney need to comply with CRC 1.201 to protect personal privacy. Only the last 4 digits of financial account numbers may be disclosed in pleadings and other papers including account statements filed with the Court (viewable to the public in PES & PTR cases unless sealed).

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF JOHN COX
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #3, REPORT, FEES
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 09/22/2020. The following issues must be addressed: 1) No statement of the US Bank accounts has been filed. 2) A statement of compensation required by Probate Code section 1064(a)(3) is missing. 3) Why hasn't the petitioner opened a regular bank account for this conservatorship? 4) The petitioner's timesheet shows he sent a check on 02/22/2019, but this payment does not appear on Schedule C or C-1. 5) Has the petitioner obtained court approval to request and withdraw distributions from the conservatee's IRA accounts? 6) Did the 12/21/2015 order authorize the petitioner to transfer future IRA distributions to the related trust? 7) Why does the San Francisco Federal Credit Union account statement include the related trust's savings account? Also, why does the statement indicate that the IRA accounts are titled under the petitioner's name as trustee of the related trust (and not as conservator of the estate)?

rulings.Case Title: **TRANSFERRED TO ALAMEDA CO.** IN THE MATTER OF THE AMARO LALAND ROMERO MINOR'S SETTLEMENT TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Directing Establishment Of Minor'S Settlement Trust, Authorizing Investment In Mutual Funds And Government Bonds, And Changing Venue And Approving Attorney'S Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Issues may include (but not limited to): Petitioner's representative capacity as guardian ad litem to seek relief related to minor's trust; establishment of minor's trust; appointment of Herb Thomas as trustee and his fees on account; bond; change of venue; and payment of preschool tuition from trust. ***Until further notice, the Court strongly encourages remote appearances by video or telephone. Counsel and parties may appear by video or audio-only telephone through Zoom. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Review the Court's 6th Amended General Order which took effect on 07/21/2020 for more information. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom. Anyone making an in-person appearance must first familiarize themselves with the Court's current pandemic-related requirements and protocols by reviewing the Court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/.
